text: Modern web development requires careful attention to security. One basic measure is using HTTPS, which encrypts data between the user and the website. This prevents attackers from stealing sensitive information like passwords. Additionally, proper authentication systems are necessary. They verify user identity through passwords or other methods, ensuring only authorized people access accounts. Another critical step is input validation—checking all data users submit. Without it, hackers can inject malicious code, leading to common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting attacks. By implementing these practices, developers can protect both users and data from harm.
